statement.en: When Prompts Override Vision: Prompt-Induced Hallucinations in LVLMs
statement.summary: Despite impressive progress in capabilities of large vision-language models (LVLMs), these systems remain vulnerable to hallucinations, i.e., outputs that are not grounded in the visual input. Prior work has attributed hallucinations in LVLMs to factors such as limitations of the vision backbone or the dominance of the language component, yet the relative importance of these factors remains unclear. To resolve this ambiguity, We propose HalluScope, a benchmark to better understand the extent to which different factors induce hallucinations. Our analysis indicates that hallucinations largely stem from excessive reliance on textual priors and background knowledge, especially information introduced through textual instructions. To mitigate hallucinations induced by textual instruction priors,

proposer: Pegah Khayatan, Jayneel Parekh, Arnaud Dapogny et al.

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The paper presents empirical findings from a 2026-dated benchmark study on LVLM hallucinations. The core claim (prompts override vision grounding) is experimentally supported but the work is positioned as incomplete ('especially information introduced through textual instructions. To mitigate...' — abstract cuts mid-sentence). Classification as FLOWING reflects: (1) provisional nature of empirical claims pending broader validation, (2) active research frontier with evolving mitigation strategies, (3) incomplete presentation suggesting preprint status.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 言語の重みが増すほど、視覚的根拠は消え去る。

visualGlyph.interpretation: テキストが視覚を侵食する——光は言葉に呑み込まれる。
